blocks.text: DTC P0792: Drive Pulley Speed Sensor Failure

The drive pulley speed sensor is mounted on the transmission case. The drive pulley speed sensor detects the rotation of the teeth on the outer circumference of the drive pulley and generates a voltage signal with a frequency that varies with rotation speed. This signal is sent to the PGM-FI ECU and used to measure the drive pulley rotation speed. The PGM-FI ECU detects missing drive pulley speed sensor pulses during drive pulley rotation as a failure. Failures are caused by open or temporary disconnections or short circuits. If there is an abnormality in the speed ratio detected by the drive pulley speed sensor and torque converter turbine speed sensor, the PGM-FI ECU judges a failure and stores a DTC.
Detection frequency, detection sequence, detection time required, detection method type, OBD status

The speed detected by the drive pulley speed sensor [drive pulley rpm] is less than the speed detected by the torque converter turbine speed sensor [torque converter turbine speed] multiplied by a factor of 0.53 for more than 15 seconds.

items.text: Ground fault in cord (NDR line) between PGM-FI ECU and drive pulley speed sensor
Disconnection of the cord (NDR line) between the PGM-FI ECU and the drive pulley speed sensor (including disconnected or loose coupler)
Disconnection of cord (VCC line) between PGM-FI ECU and drive pulley speed sensor (including disconnected or loose coupler)
Disconnection of cord (SG line) between PGM-FI ECU and drive pulley speed sensor (including disconnected or loose coupler)
Vehicle speed [vehicle speed] 30 km/h or more for 30 seconds or more.
When conducting test drives, the driver must comply with all laws and regulations, observe good manners, and drive in accordance with the surrounding conditions.

blocks.text: If the vehicle is determined to be faulty, the D indicator light flashes without the PGM-FI warning light illuminating, and the temporary DTC, DTC and freeze data are stored in the PGM-FI ECU memory.
Use of the clear command of the scan tool (including HDS) or removal of the 12V battery terminal will clear the D indicator light, temporary DTC, DTC, and freeze data.
